California
Fair POlitical
Practices Cornmlssron
November 6, L99o
Donal-d J. Burnett, President
California Police chiefs Associati'on Inc.
1485 River Park Dr., Suite 20o
Sacramento, cA 95815
Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
our File No. I-9o-637
Dear Mr. Burnett:
This tetter is in response to your request for advice regard-
inq the conf l ict-of- interest provisions of the Political 
Reform
acf (tne "Act").1 At your request, we are providing you with
infoimal assistance pursuant to the provisions of Regulation
18329(c) (copy enclolea1 .z we do not provide advice concerning
provisions oi-law other than the Act. You may wish to consult
iitn tfr" Attorney GeneraL's office regarding other provisions of
law such as section 1090 which prohibits government officials 
from
having an interest in contracts with their agencies.
OUESTION
Under the provisions of the Act, may a loca1 police depart-
ment enter into contracts for required police training services
with a company which has been a source of $250 or more in income
to the chief of Police?
CONCLUSION
Under the provisions of the Act, a local pol ice department
may enter into Lontracts for required pol-ice training services
wittr a company which has been a source of S250 or more in income
to the ctriet of police. 
However, the chief of police is
disqualified from participating in or attenpting to influence the
1 Go.r".rr."nt code Sections 810oo-91015- A1I statutory references
are to the covernment Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Commission
regulations appear at 2 california code of Regulations section
18ooo, et seq'- A11 references to regulations are to Title 2,
Oivision e of ttre California code of Regulations'
2 Inforrna.l- assistance does not provide the requestor with the
irnnunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice' 
(Sec-
tion 83114; Regulation 18329 (c) - )
428 J Street, Suite 8OO o P.O. Box 8O7 o Sacramento CA 958O4-O8O7 o (916) 322.i66O


our File No. I-90-637
Page 2
decision. P1ease note that Government code section 1o9o may be
applicable to your facts. 
If applicable, Section l'09o could
prohibit the contract in questj-on.
FACTS
Members of the california Po1ice Chiefs Association Inc.
comprise a significant number of the instructors for P-o-s-T. ap-
proved peace officer training courses. As president of the
-california Police Chiefs Association Inc. you wish to advise the
nembers of the association regarding potential conflicts of inter-
est under the Act. In that capacity, you seek our advice to
determine whether a loca1 potice department nay enter into
contracts for required police traininq services with a conpany
which has been a source of income to the chief of police.
The Departnent of Justice Cornnission on Peace officer
Standards and Training (P.o.s.T.) has certified the particular
conpany as a provider of continuing education and required police
training. 
The chief of police, a court qualified training expert
and one of two instructors certified by the State of california to
teach a certain P.o.S.T. reimbursed course, was enployed by the
company within the last 12 months to teach the course at a rate of
pay- eslablished by P.o.s.T. The potice chief ha-s received $250 or
iloie in income frorn this company within the preceding twelve
rnonths. The company is certified by P.O-S.T. as the exclusive
provider of this particular course.
The chief of police is not directly involved in the selection
or assigTnment of training courses for his department. These func-
tions aie delegated to the police department's training unit. 
The
police department has contracted with the comPany to provide
training and prornotional testing prior to the current police
chief's adninistration. 
The scope of the conpany's involvement
with the city in providing contemplated future courses could
include both P.o.s.T. certified reinbursable courses as well as
training not to be reirnbursed to the Police department by the
state. 
Many P.o.s.T. courses require the exclusive expertise of
police chieis which is not cornmonly available in the private sec-
Lor. Under no circunstances would the chief of police be ernployed
for any non-certified reimbursed training nor would the chief of
police be involved in decisions which would affect the company.
ANALYSI S
Section 87100 prohibits a Pubtic official 
fron naking,
participating in making, or in any rray. attenpting to influence a
|overnmLntal 
- decisj.on in which the official 
has a financial inter-
6st. 
The term 'rpublic officialr' includes all nembers, officers,
ernployees, or consultants of a state or Ioca1 government agency.
(section 82048 | Regulation 18700(a), coPY enclosed.)


Our File No. I-9 0-63 7
Page 3
section 82041 defines "Iocal governnent agency'r as a county'
city, or district 
of any kind, including riany department, divi-
sion, bureau, office, board, commission, or other agency of the
foregoing.rr Generally, the comrnission has treated different city
depaitnents as different agencies. For example, a planning com-
nilsion is a distinct agency from a park and recreation commis-
sion. (stout Advice Letter, No. I-88-313, coPy enclosed.) A
police department, then, is also a distinct agency within the city
lnd the chief of potice is a public official. 
(section 82048.)
An official 
has a financial interest in a decision if it is
reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a rnaterial
financial effect, distinguishable from its effect on the public
generally, on the official, 
or a rnember of the official's 
irnrnedi-
ate family, or on:
(a) Any business entity in which the public
official 
has a direct or indirect investment worth
one thousand dollars ($1,000) or more.
(b) Any real property in which the public
official 
has a direct or indirect interest worth
one thousand dollars ($1,000) or nore.
(c) Any source of incone, other than gifts
and other than loans by a conmercial fendinq
institution in the reg"uLar course of business on
terms available to the public without regard to
official 
status, aggEeqating two hundred fifty 
do1-
lars ($250) or Dore in value provided to, received
by or promised to the public official within 12
nonths prior to the tine when the decision is made.
(d) Any business entity in which the public
official 
is a director, officer, Partner, trustee,
employee, or holds any position of management.
(e) Any donor of, or any internediary or
agent for a donor of, a gift or gifts aggregating
two hundred fifty dollars ($250) or more in value
provided to, received by, or prornised to the public
officiaf within 12 months prior to the time when
the decision is made.
For purposes of this section, indirect invest-
rnent or interest means any investment or interest
olrned by the spouse or dependent child of a public
official, 
by an agent on behalf of a public of-
ficial, 
or by a business entity or trust in which
the official, 
the official's 
agents, spouse, and
dependent children own directly, indirectly, 
or
beneficially a 1O-percent interest or greater.
section 87■03.


our File No. I-90-6 3 7
Page 4
Therefore, the police chief is disqualified from participating in
a decision that will have a foreseeable and naterial financial
effect on any of his economic interests.
Foreseeabi litv
The effects of a decision are reasonably foreseeable if there
is a substantial likelihood that they will occur. To be foresee-
abIe, the effects of a decision must be nore than a nere possibil-
ity; however certainty is not required. 
(Downev cares v. Downev
Communitv Development Con. (1987) 196 CaI.App.3d 983, 9a9-991;
Witt v. Morro!, (1977) 70 CaL.ApP.3d 817,' In re Thorner (1975) 1
FPPC ops. 198 (copy enclosed) . ) The Act seeks to prevent more
than attual conflicts of j,nterest. It seeks to Prevent even the
appearance of a possible conftict of interest. 
(witt v. tltorrow,
supra at 823. )
In this instance, it is foreseeable that the decision to
contract for police training will have an effect on the company
which is the Lxclusive provider of the service. Because this
company has been a source of income of $250 or more to the chief
of iolice in the preceding twelve months, the chief of police nay
not participate in the decision if the effect of the decision upon
the company r,ritl be material.
Materia l itv
The effect of a decision is deemed material lrhen a source of
incorne to a public officiat 
is directly involved in a decision
before the oificial's 
agency or there is a nexus betlreen the
purpose for which the official 
receives incorne and the
lovLrnnental decision. 
(Regmlation La7o2-1(a), coPy enclosed.) A
iusiness entity is directly involved in a decision before an
official's 
agency when that business entity, either personally or
by an agent:
(1) Initiates the proceedinq in which the
decision wifl be nade by filing an application,
c1aim, apPeal, or similar reguest or;
(2) Is a named party in, or is the subject
of, the proceeding concerning the decision before
the official 
or the offi,cial's 
alrency.
(3) A person or business entity is the
subject of a proceeding if a decision involves the
issuance, renewal, approval, denial or revocation
of any license, permit, or other entitlenent to, or
contract with, the subject person or business
entity.
Regnrlation 18702. 1 (b) .


our File No. I-90-537
Page 5
The decision to provide training to the police force through
this particular company involves entering into a contract with
this Lornpany, Therefore, this cornpany is directly before the
official's 
agency. Because this conpany has been a source of
income of at least 9250 to the chief of police in the twelve
nonths precedinq the decision, the chief of police is disqualified
fron parti.cipating in the decision.
It is also foreseeable that the decision to contract for this
service will have an effect on the chief of police. 
Since he is
one of two providers of a particular course, there is a
substantial likelihood that the decision to contract for the
services of comPany X will result in the enployment of the chief
of police to teach the particular training course- - The effect of
=lr"ir . decision is material as to the chief of police if :
The decision wilf result in the personal'
expenses, incorne, assets (other than interest in
rell property), or tiabilities 
of the official 
or
hj,s or her irnmediate faroiIy increasing or decreas-
ing by at least $250.
Regulation ]-a'l02.2 (a) (4), coPY
enclosed.
The chief of police is disqualified from naking, participat-
ing in making, or using his official .position to influence deci-
si6ns to employ company X if the decisions will naterially affect
company X or tfre chief of police. Disqualification means that the
cniLf of police cannot participate in discussions, vote on the
natter, oi otherwise try to infLuence members of his aqency.
(Section 871oOi Regulations 18700 and 18700.1; Levinqer Advice
ietter, No. I-88-3r8; west Advice Letter, No. A-88-413, copies
enclosed.) Prohibited attenpts to influence a decision include
contacting any member, officer, or employee of the official's
agency. (negutation 18700.1(a).) Therefore, the chief of police
miy n-ot participate in the selection or assignment of training
"oi,.="= ior his- dePartment, attenPt to influence the selection of
a provi.der for sucl services, or authorize any decision nade by
hii staff with regard to the enptoynent of this company to provide
police training services.


Our FiIe No. I-9 0-537
Page 6
Additionally, other provisions of law such as Section 10903
rnay be applicable to the situation you address. The commission
has no jurisdiction to interpret or enforce section 1090. The
cornmission's jurisdiction is linited to the Political Reform Act.
Questions concerning section 1090 should be addressed to the At-
torney General's office.
we trust this letter adequately responds to your inquiry.
should you have any further questions regarding this rnatter, do
not hesitate to call ne at (916) 322-5901.
S incerely,
scott Hallabrin
Acting ceneral counsel
By: Blanca lil. Breeze
counsel,, Legal Division
SH: BMB: PIh
Enclosures
Section 1090 states:
Menbers of the Legislature, state, county,
district, judicial district, and city officers
or employees shall not be financially interested
in any contract nade by then in their official
capacity, or by any body or board of rrhich they
are nenbers. Nor shall state, county, district,
judiciat district, and city officers or enployees
be purchasers at any sal,e or vendors at any
purchase made by thern in their official capacity.
As used in this article, rrdistrictrr neans
agency of the state forned pursuant to general
Iaw or special act, for the local performance
of governnental or proprietary functions within
Iimited boundaries.
